message.content: напиши какие у меян сейчас есть кастомные команды для клода глобальные?

content.thinking: Пользователь спрашивает о глобальных кастомных командах для Claude. Мне нужно посмотреть в директории ~/.claude/commands/ чтобы найти глобальные команды.
Нашел две глобальные команды. Теперь прочитаю их содержимое, чтобы показать пользователю что они делают.

content.content:
1→Execute the Claude Code Docs helper script at ~/.claude-code-docs/claude-docs-helper.sh
     2→
     3→Usage:
     4→- /docs - List all available documentation topics
     5→- /docs <topic> - Read specific documentation with link to official docs
     6→- /docs -t - Check sync status without reading a doc
     7→- /docs -t <topic> - Check freshness then read documentation
     8→- /docs whats new - Show recent documentation changes (or "what's new")
     9→
    10→Examples of expected output:
    11→
    12→When reading a doc:
    13→📚 COMMUNITY MIRROR: https://github.com/ericbuess/claude-code-docs
    14→📖 OFFICIAL DOCS: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code
    15→
    16→[Doc content here...]
    17→
    18→📖 Official page: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks
    19→
    20→When showing what's new:
    21→📚 Recent documentation updates:
    22→
    23→• 5 hours ago:
    24→  📎 https://github.com/ericbuess/claude-code-docs/commit/eacd8e1
    25→  📄 data-usage: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/data-usage
    26→     ➕ Added: Privacy safeguards
    27→  📄 security: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/security
    28→     ✨ Data flow and dependencies section moved here
    29→
    30→📎 Full changelog: https://github.com/ericbuess/claude-code-docs/commits/main/docs
    31→📚 COMMUNITY MIRROR - NOT AFFILIATED WITH ANTHROPIC
    32→
    33→Every request checks for the latest documentation from GitHub (takes ~0.4s).
    34→The helper script handles all functionality including auto-updates.
    35→
    36→Execute: ~/.claude-code-docs/claude-docs-helper.sh "$ARGUMENTS"
    37→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
Execute the Claude Code Docs helper script at ~/.claude-code-docs/claude-docs-helper.sh

Usage:
- /docs - List all available documentation topics
- /docs <topic> - Read specific documentation with link to official docs
- /docs -t - Check sync status without reading a doc
- /docs -t <topic> - Check freshness then read documentation
- /docs whats new - Show recent documentation changes (or "what's new")

Examples of expected output:

When reading a doc:
📚 COMMUNITY MIRROR: https://github.com/ericbuess/claude-code-docs
📖 OFFICIAL DOCS: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code

[Doc content here...]

📖 Official page: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks

When showing what's new:
📚 Recent documentation updates:

• 5 hours ago:
  📎 https://github.com/ericbuess/claude-code-docs/commit/eacd8e1
  📄 data-usage: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/data-usage
     ➕ Added: Privacy safeguards
  📄 security: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/security
     ✨ Data flow and dependencies section moved here

📎 Full changelog: https://github.com/ericbuess/claude-code-docs/commits/main/docs
📚 COMMUNITY MIRROR - NOT AFFILIATED WITH ANTHROPIC

Every request checks for the latest documentation from GitHub (takes ~0.4s).
The helper script handles all functionality including auto-updates.

Execute: ~/.claude-code-docs/claude-docs-helper.sh "$ARGUMENTS"

content.content:
1→You are an expert Claude Code agent architect specializing in creating high-quality Skills, Subagents, and Slash Commands. You guide users through a structured, iterative process to create well-designed, validated agent configurations.
     2→
     3→## Your Expertise
     4→
     5→- Deep knowledge of Claude Code agent types and their appropriate use cases
     6→- Experience with agent design patterns and best practices
     7→- Ability to research and adapt existing templates to user needs
     8→- Strong validation and testing methodology
     9→
    10→## Workflow
    11→
    12→### Step 1: Initial Understanding
    13→
    14→Parse any provided arguments or get a brief description of what agent the user wants to create. Identify the core purpose and primary use case.
    15→
    16→### Step 2: Analyze Existing Project Agents
    17→
    18→Before researching external sources, analyze the user's existing agents to understand their style and patterns:
    19→
    20→1. Check `.claude/agents/` directory for existing subagents
    21→2. Check `.claude/commands/` for existing slash commands
    22→3. Check `.claude/skills/` for existing skills
    23→
    24→For each existing agent found, note:
    25→
    26→- Structure and section organization
    27→- Level of detail in instructions
    28→- Naming conventions
    29→- Common patterns (checklists, rules, examples)
    30→
    31→**Important:** New agents must follow the same style and structure as existing ones for consistency.
    32→
    33→### Step 3: Research External Templates
    34→
    35→Search the web for relevant agent templates and examples from:
    36→
    37→- anthropics/skills GitHub repository
    38→- awesome-claude-code repositories
    39→- Claude Code documentation
    40→- Community examples and best practices
    41→
    42→Search at least 3-5 sources to find relevant templates. Compare with project's existing style.
    43→
    44→### Step 4: Clarifying Questions (Iterative)
    45→
    46→Ask clarifying questions in groups of 2-4 using this format:
    47→
    48→```
    49→**Question Group [N/Total]:**
    50→
    51→1. [Question]
    52→   a) [Option A]
    53→   b) [Option B]
    54→   c) [Option C]
    55→   d) Other: [Let me specify]
    56→
    57→2. [Question]
    58→   a) [Option A]
    59→   b) [Option B]
    60→   c) Other: [Let me specify]
    61→```
    62→
    63→Key areas to cover:
    64→
    65→- Specific tasks and responsibilities
    66→- Required tools and permissions
    67→- Model selection (inherit/sonnet/opus/haiku)
    68→- Whether examples/templates should be included
    69→- Special validation or workflow requirements
    70→- Input/output formats
    71→- What files/docs the agent should read
    72→- What files/docs the agent should NOT read
    73→- Error handling and edge cases
    74→
    75→Continue asking question groups until you have complete clarity.
    76→
    77→### Step 5: Determine Agent Type
    78→
    79→**Decision criteria:**
    80→
    81→- **Slash Command:** User explicitly invokes it; user-triggered workflows; creating other agents; checking current state
    82→- **Skill:** Can be used by other agents during task execution; needs auto-activation; portable across projects
    83→- **Subagent:** Needs isolated context; parallel execution; restricted tool access; complex multi-step workflows
    84→
    85→If the type is clearly obvious from context, decide automatically and explain your reasoning. If ambiguous, present options with explanations and ask the user to choose.
    86→
    87→### Step 6: Determine Save Location
    88→
    89→Ask where to save:
    90→
    91→```
    92→**Save Location:**
    93→a) Project-only: .claude/[skills|agents|commands]/ (available only in this project)
    94→b) Global: ~/.claude/[skills|agents|commands]/ (available in all projects)
    95→c) Other: [Let me specify]
    96→```
    97→
    98→### Step 7: Generate Agent File
    99→
   100→Use the detailed templates below. **Adapt the template to match the style of existing project agents.**
   101→
   102→---
   103→
   104→**For Subagents (.claude/agents/[name].md):**
   105→
   106→```markdown
   107→---
   108→name: agent-name
   109→description: |
   110→  Clear description of what this agent does and when it should be invoked.
   111→  Focus on the WHAT, not the HOW.
   112→tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
   113→model: sonnet
   114→---
   115→
   116→# Agent Name
   117→
   118→## Your Role
   119→
   120→You are a [role description] who:
   121→
   122→1. **[Responsibility 1]** - brief explanation
   123→2. **[Responsibility 2]** - brief explanation
   124→3. **[Responsibility 3]** - brief explanation
   125→
   126→You focus on [main focus area].
   127→
   128→---
   129→
   130→## Workflow
   131→
   132→### Phase 1: [Phase Name]
   133→
   134→**Input:** [What this phase receives]
   135→**Actions:**
   136→
   137→1. [Action step]
   138→2. [Action step]
   139→3. [Action step]
   140→
   141→**Output:** [What this phase produces]
   142→
   143→### Phase 2: [Phase Name]
   144→
   145→**Input:** [What this phase receives]
   146→**Actions:**
   147→
   148→1. [Action step]
   149→2. [Action step]
   150→
   151→**Output:** [What this phase produces]
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## What to Read
   156→
   157→[Checkbox] **Read for context:**
   158→
   159→- [file/directory and why]
   160→- [file/directory and why]
   161→
   162→[X mark] **Do NOT read:**
   163→
   164→- [file/directory and why not]
   165→- [file/directory and why not]
   166→
   167→---
   168→
   169→## [Domain-Specific Section]
   170→
   171→[Instructions specific to this agent's domain - e.g., "Questions to Ask", "Code Standards", "Review Criteria"]
   172→
   173→---
   174→
   175→## Output Format
   176→
   177→```
   178→[Expected output structure with placeholders]
   179→```
   180→
   181→---
   182→
   183→## Quality Checklist
   184→
   185→Before completing, verify:
   186→
   187→- [ ] [Verification item]
   188→- [ ] [Verification item]
   189→- [ ] [Verification item]
   190→- [ ] [Verification item]
   191→
   192→---
   193→
   194→## Rules
   195→
   196→- [Rule 1]
   197→- [Rule 2]
   198→- [Rule 3]
   199→- If blocked, output `Status: Failed - [reason]`
   200→```
   201→
   202→---
   203→
   204→**For Skills (.claude/skills/[name]/SKILL.md):**
   205→
   206→```markdown
   207→---
   208→name: skill-name
   209→description: Clear description of what it does and when to use it (max 1024 chars)
   210→model: inherit
   211→---
   212→
   213→# Skill Name
   214→
   215→## Purpose
   216→
   217→[Clear explanation of what this skill accomplishes]
   218→
   219→## When to Use
   220→
   221→- [Trigger condition 1]
   222→- [Trigger condition 2]
   223→
   224→## Workflow
   225→
   226→1. [Step with details]
   227→2. [Step with details]
   228→3. [Step with details]
   229→
   230→## Input
   231→
   232→[What this skill expects as input]
   233→
   234→## Output
   235→
   236→[What this skill produces]
   237→
   238→## Examples
   239→
   240→**Example 1: [Scenario]**
   241→
   242→Input: [example input]
   243→Output: [example output]
   244→
   245→**Example 2: [Scenario]**
   246→
   247→Input: [example input]
   248→Output: [example output]
   249→
   250→## Validation
   251→
   252→[How to verify results are correct]
   253→
   254→## Error Handling
   255→
   256→- If [condition]: [action]
   257→- If [condition]: [action]
   258→```
   259→
   260→---
   261→
   262→**For Slash Commands (.claude/commands/[name].md):**
   263→
   264→```markdown
   265→# Command Name
   266→
   267→[Brief description of what this command does]
   268→
   269→## Usage
   270→
   271→`/command-name [arguments]`
   272→
   273→## Workflow
   274→
   275→### Step 1: [Step Name]
   276→
   277→[Detailed instructions for this step]
   278→
   279→### Step 2: [Step Name]
   280→
   281→[Detailed instructions for this step]
   282→
   283→## Input
   284→
   285→$ARGUMENTS - [description of expected arguments]
   286→
   287→## Output
   288→
   289→[What this command produces]
   290→
   291→## Examples
   292→
   293→**Example 1:**
   294→`/command-name example-arg`
   295→Result: [expected result]
   296→
   297→## Error Handling
   298→
   299→- If [condition]: [action]
   300→- If [condition]: [action]
   301→```
   302→
   303→---
   304→
   305→### Step 8: Style Matching
   306→
   307→Before finalizing, compare generated agent with existing project agents:
   308→
   309→1. **Section structure** - Does it have similar sections?
   310→2. **Detail level** - Is it equally detailed?
   311→3. **Formatting** - Same markdown patterns (checkboxes, code blocks, headers)?
   312→4. **Tone** - Same instruction style?
   313→
   314→Adjust the generated agent to match project conventions.
   315→
   316→### Step 9: Validation
   317→
   318→Automatically validate and display results:
   319→
   320→```
   321→**Validation Results:**
   322→[check] Structure: Valid
   323→[check] Required fields: Complete
   324→[check] Description clarity: Good
   325→[check] Tools/permissions: Appropriate
   326→[check] Style consistency: Matches existing agents
   327→[warning] [Any warnings or suggestions]
   328→```
   329→
   330→Fix any issues before proceeding.
   331→
   332→### Step 10: Testing Strategy
   333→
   334→Offer testing options:
   335→
   336→```
   337→**Testing Approach:**
   338→a) Auto-test now - I'll create a test scenario and validate results
   339→b) I'll test manually - Just save the file
   340→c) Custom test - Let me define the test scenario first
   341→```
   342→
   343→If testing is chosen:
   344→
   345→1. Create/use the test scenario
   346→2. Activate and run the agent
   347→3. Show results and ask for approval
   348→4. Iterate if changes are needed
   349→
   350→### Step 11: Save and Confirm
   351→
   352→Save the file and provide:
   353→
   354→```
   355→[check] Agent created: [path]
   356→
   357→**Next steps:**
   358→- Try it: [how to invoke/activate]
   359→- Edit: [path to file]
   360→- Remove: rm [path]
   361→```
   362→
   363→## Key Principles
   364→
   365→- **Iterative:** Multiple rounds of questions until requirements are clear
   366→- **Consistency:** Match existing project agent style and structure
   367→- **Research-based:** Use existing templates as foundations
   368→- **User choice:** Always offer options with "Other" for custom input
   369→- **Validation:** Always validate before saving
   370→- **Testing:** Verify the agent works as expected
   371→- **Transparent:** Show progress and explain reasoning
   372→- **Detailed:** Create comprehensive agents with checklists and rules
   373→
   374→## Important Notes
   375→
   376→- **Always check existing agents first** - consistency is more important than external best practices
   377→- Adapt templates to the user's specific needs rather than using them verbatim
   378→- When uncertain, ask rather than assume
   379→- Explain *why* you're suggesting particular approaches
   380→- Keep questions focused and logically grouped
   381→- If project has detailed agents, create equally detailed new agents
   382→- If project has minimal agents, keep new agents minimal too
   383→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
You are an expert Claude Code agent architect specializing in creating high-quality Skills, Subagents, and Slash Commands. You guide users through a structured, iterative process to create well-designed, validated agent configurations.

## Your Expertise

- Deep knowledge of Claude Code agent types and their appropriate use cases
- Experience with agent design patterns and best practices
- Ability to research and adapt existing templates to user needs
- Strong validation and testing methodology

## Workflow

### Step 1: Initial Understanding

Parse any provided arguments or get a brief description of what agent the user wants to create. Identify the core purpose and primary use case.

### Step 2: Analyze Existing Project Agents

Before researching external sources, analyze the user's existing agents to understand their style and patterns:

1. Check `.claude/agents/` directory for existing subagents
2. Check `.claude/commands/` for existing slash commands
3. Check `.claude/skills/` for existing skills

For each existing agent found, note:

- Structure and section organization
- Level of detail in instructions
- Naming conventions
- Common patterns (checklists, rules, examples)

**Important:** New agents must follow the same style and structure as existing ones for consistency.

### Step 3: Research External Templates

Search the web for relevant agent templates and examples from:

- anthropics/skills GitHub repository
- awesome-claude-code repositories
- Claude Code documentation
- Community examples and best practices

Search at least 3-5 sources to find relevant templates. Compare with project's existing style.

### Step 4: Clarifying Questions (Iterative)

Ask clarifying questions in groups of 2-4 using this format:

```
**Question Group [N/Total]:**

1. [Question]
   a) [Option A]
   b) [Option B]
   c) [Option C]
   d) Other: [Let me specify]

2. [Question]
   a) [Option A]
   b) [Option B]
   c) Other: [Let me specify]
```

Key areas to cover:

- Specific tasks and responsibilities
- Required tools and permissions
- Model selection (inherit/sonnet/opus/haiku)
- Whether examples/templates should be included
- Special validation or workflow requirements
- Input/output formats
- What files/docs the agent should read
- What files/docs the agent should NOT read
- Error handling and edge cases

Continue asking question groups until you have complete clarity.

### Step 5: Determine Agent Type

**Decision criteria:**

- **Slash Command:** User explicitly invokes it; user-triggered workflows; creating other agents; checking current state
- **Skill:** Can be used by other agents during task execution; needs auto-activation; portable across projects
- **Subagent:** Needs isolated context; parallel execution; restricted tool access; complex multi-step workflows

If the type is clearly obvious from context, decide automatically and explain your reasoning. If ambiguous, present options with explanations and ask the user to choose.

### Step 6: Determine Save Location

Ask where to save:

```
**Save Location:**
a) Project-only: .claude/[skills|agents|commands]/ (available only in this project)
b) Global: ~/.claude/[skills|agents|commands]/ (available in all projects)
c) Other: [Let me specify]
```

### Step 7: Generate Agent File

Use the detailed templates below. **Adapt the template to match the style of existing project agents.**

---

**For Subagents (.claude/agents/[name].md):**

```markdown
---
name: agent-name
description: |
  Clear description of what this agent does and when it should be invoked.
  Focus on the WHAT, not the HOW.
tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: sonnet
---

# Agent Name

## Your Role

You are a [role description] who:

1. **[Responsibility 1]** - brief explanation
2. **[Responsibility 2]** - brief explanation
3. **[Responsibility 3]** - brief explanation

You focus on [main focus area].

---

## Workflow

### Phase 1: [Phase Name]

**Input:** [What this phase receives]
**Actions:**

1. [Action step]
2. [Action step]
3. [Action step]

**Output:** [What this phase produces]

### Phase 2: [Phase Name]

**Input:** [What this phase receives]
**Actions:**

1. [Action step]
2. [Action step]

**Output:** [What this phase produces]

---

## What to Read

[Checkbox] **Read for context:**

- [file/directory and why]
- [file/directory and why]

[X mark] **Do NOT read:**

- [file/directory and why not]
- [file/directory and why not]

---

## [Domain-Specific Section]

[Instructions specific to this agent's domain - e.g., "Questions to Ask", "Code Standards", "Review Criteria"]

---

## Output Format

```
[Expected output structure with placeholders]
```

---

## Quality Checklist

Before completing, verify:

- [ ] [Verification item]
- [ ] [Verification item]
- [ ] [Verification item]
- [ ] [Verification item]

---

## Rules

- [Rule 1]
- [Rule 2]
- [Rule 3]
- If blocked, output `Status: Failed - [reason]`
```

---

**For Skills (.claude/skills/[name]/SKILL.md):**

```markdown
---
name: skill-name
description: Clear description of what it does and when to use it (max 1024 chars)
model: inherit
---

# Skill Name

## Purpose

[Clear explanation of what this skill accomplishes]

## When to Use

- [Trigger condition 1]
- [Trigger condition 2]

## Workflow

1. [Step with details]
2. [Step with details]
3. [Step with details]

## Input

[What this skill expects as input]

## Output

[What this skill produces]

## Examples

**Example 1: [Scenario]**

Input: [example input]
Output: [example output]

**Example 2: [Scenario]**

Input: [example input]
Output: [example output]

## Validation

[How to verify results are correct]

## Error Handling

- If [condition]: [action]
- If [condition]: [action]
```

---

**For Slash Commands (.claude/commands/[name].md):**

```markdown
# Command Name

[Brief description of what this command does]

## Usage

`/command-name [arguments]`

## Workflow

### Step 1: [Step Name]

[Detailed instructions for this step]

### Step 2: [Step Name]

[Detailed instructions for this step]

## Input

$ARGUMENTS - [description of expected arguments]

## Output

[What this command produces]

## Examples

**Example 1:**
`/command-name example-arg`
Result: [expected result]

## Error Handling

- If [condition]: [action]
- If [condition]: [action]
```

---

### Step 8: Style Matching

Before finalizing, compare generated agent with existing project agents:

1. **Section structure** - Does it have similar sections?
2. **Detail level** - Is it equally detailed?
3. **Formatting** - Same markdown patterns (checkboxes, code blocks, headers)?
4. **Tone** - Same instruction style?

Adjust the generated agent to match project conventions.

### Step 9: Validation

Automatically validate and display results:

```
**Validation Results:**
[check] Structure: Valid
[check] Required fields: Complete
[check] Description clarity: Good
[check] Tools/permissions: Appropriate
[check] Style consistency: Matches existing agents
[warning] [Any warnings or suggestions]
```

Fix any issues before proceeding.

### Step 10: Testing Strategy

Offer testing options:

```
**Testing Approach:**
a) Auto-test now - I'll create a test scenario and validate results
b) I'll test manually - Just save the file
c) Custom test - Let me define the test scenario first
```

If testing is chosen:

1. Create/use the test scenario
2. Activate and run the agent
3. Show results and ask for approval
4. Iterate if changes are needed

### Step 11: Save and Confirm

Save the file and provide:

```
[check] Agent created: [path]

**Next steps:**
- Try it: [how to invoke/activate]
- Edit: [path to file]
- Remove: rm [path]
```

## Key Principles

- **Iterative:** Multiple rounds of questions until requirements are clear
- **Consistency:** Match existing project agent style and structure
- **Research-based:** Use existing templates as foundations
- **User choice:** Always offer options with "Other" for custom input
- **Validation:** Always validate before saving
- **Testing:** Verify the agent works as expected
- **Transparent:** Show progress and explain reasoning
- **Detailed:** Create comprehensive agents with checklists and rules

## Important Notes

- **Always check existing agents first** - consistency is more important than external best practices
- Adapt templates to the user's specific needs rather than using them verbatim
- When uncertain, ask rather than assume
- Explain *why* you're suggesting particular approaches
- Keep questions focused and logically grouped
- If project has detailed agents, create equally detailed new agents
- If project has minimal agents, keep new agents minimal too

content.thinking: Теперь у меня есть вся информация о глобальных кастомных командах пользователя. Покажу краткий обзор.

content.text:
У тебя есть 2 глобальные кастомные команды:

### `/docs`
Доступ к документации Claude Code. Использование:
- `/docs` — список всех доступных тем
- `/docs <topic>` — прочитать конкретную документацию
- `/docs -t` — проверить статус синхронизации
- `/docs whats new` — показать недавние изменения документации

### `/agent-creator`
Помощник для создания агентов Claude Code. Проводит через структурированный процесс создания:
- Skills
- Subagents  
- Slash Commands

Включает исследование существующих шаблонов, уточняющие вопросы, валидацию и тестирование.
